summary: Iter-49 verified the new /api/analytics/kpi-trend bucketed endpoint and the KPI Trend chart drill up/down + single-line refactor. All 7 backend pytests PASSED in 11.74s (day/week/month/quarter buckets, non-zero returns/discount/abv regression check, daily-sum vs /kpis tolerance, CSV country sums = single-country sum). All 4 frontend assertions PASSED: chart renders single line (line_groups=1), all 4 bucket buttons (Daily/Weekly/Monthly/Quarterly) are clickable, switching to Returns/Monthly returns axis ticks ['May 2026','0','2K','3K','5K','6K'] (non-zero peak ~5K), Targets-page 'Detailed target breakdown' card no longer has 'Projection vs Target' eyebrow, while 'Gap to Target' KPI tile + channel breakdown + quarterly progress tables remain intact. No console pageerrors.

issues: Recharts emits ~12 console warnings 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on initial mount (likely chart container measured before first paint). Cosmetic — chart renders correctly after measurement settles. Could be silenced by setting an explicit minWidth/minHeight on the parent or rendering inside a useLayoutEffect-guarded ResponsiveContainer.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py — get_kpi_trend() at line 882 fans out via asyncio.gather over up to 400 buckets calling get_kpis() in parallel; observed 12-month monthly query completes in ~2-3s in tests, well under the 10s SLA. Bound is reasonable.

server.py — _gen_kpi_trend_buckets() correctly intersects partial weeks/months/quarters at range edges (seg_start = max(period_start, df), seg_end = min(period_end, dt)). Verified via the week-bucket label/intersection test.

KpiTrendChart.jsx — clean single-line refactor; defaultBucketFor() heuristic (≤45d=day, ≤120d=week, ≤540d=month, else=quarter) is sensible and overridable. Reset link only appears when user has overridden auto-default — good UX.
Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warning during initial mount is a known recharts pattern; consider wrapping ResponsiveContainer in a useLayoutEffect or setting minHeight on the parent card to silence.
AnnualTargetsCard.jsx variant='full' header now contains only the left-side title block (lines 94-102). The removed Projection vs Target right block is no longer in the file — correct.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New endpoint /api/analytics/kpi-trend (server.py L882) is verified working for all 4 buckets. Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_49_kpi_trend.py cover: 3-day Kenya daily returns 3 rows, non-zero returns/discount/abv (regression), daily 1-month count + /kpis tolerance check, week labels 'Wk MMM DD', month labels 'MMM YYYY', quarter labels 'Q# YYYY', CSV country = single-country sum. Frontend KpiTrendChart at /app/frontend/src/components/KpiTrendChart.jsx renders single line, has 4 bucket buttons with data-testid='kpi-trend-bucket-{day|week|month|quarter}', kpi dropdown with data-testid='kpi-trend-select'. AnnualTargetsCard variant='full' top-right 'Projection vs Target' eyebrow has been removed cleanly — verified in DOM. Recharts SVG <text> elements MUST be queried via page.evaluate (not Playwright .all_text_contents) — the locator returns empty for Recharts axis labels.
rca of the issue: Backend RCA: previous KpiTrendChart hit /api/analytics/daily-trend which only exposed total_sales/net_sales/units_sold/orders, so Returns/Discount/ABV always rendered as flat-zero. Fix: new /api/analytics/kpi-trend endpoint splits the requested window into day/week/month/quarter slices via _gen_kpi_trend_buckets() and parallel-fans get_kpis() per bucket via asyncio.gather, then maps the rich KPI payload (including total_returns, total_discounts, avg_basket_size) to the response. Front-end KpiTrendChart fully rewritten: single line via dataKey='value' (chartData maps r[kpi]→value), bucket selector defaulting via defaultBucketFor(), country prop drilled from global filter bar (no per-country fan-out). Targets-page regression: AnnualTargetsCard.jsx variant='full' previously had a right-column 'Projection vs Target' eyebrow + percentage block in the header (lines ~92-111 area) which duplicated the OVERALL donut tile shown in the row above. The header has been simplified to only show the left-side annual-target title block.
